LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOSEPH ALBANESE INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S UNVERIFIED AMENDED COMPLAINT. A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : GRANTED AS TO THE ATTORNEY'S FEES REQUEST AND DENIED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND REMEDIAL CONDUCT. THE COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT ACTED IN CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE SAFETY OF BICYCLISTS. FURTHER INFORMATION CAN BE OBTAINED THROUGH DISCOVERY. THE REMEDIAL CONDUCT ALLEGATION IS POTENTIALLY RELEVANT FOR PURPOSES OTHER THAN TO PROVE NEGLIGENCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - CHRIS NOLAN OF BRENT, FIOL & NOLAN APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MERRILEE C. MILLER OF STONE & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. (925) 938-1555 EXT. 13 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE OPPOSING COUNSEL AND THE COURT AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JOHN DIONISIO'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amendment To Complaint - Plaintiff's motion for leave to file second amendment to the complaint is granted pursuant to CCP Section 474. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a second amendment to complaint to add Freyer & Laureta, Inc. as Doe 2 is granted. Plaintiff did not unreasonably delay in bringing this motion. Defendant Joseph J. Albanese, Inc. will not be unduly prejudiced. The motion is granted without prejudice to any party seeking a trial continuance. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Mtn To Strike; Req For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Oct-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID L. FIOL OF BRENT, FIOL & NOLAN LLP, TEL. NO. 259-4420 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JENNIFER K. STINNETT, TEL. NO. (310) 222-5744 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT JOSEPH ALBANESE;
ATTY. MERRILEE MILLER OF STONE & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. TEL. NO. (925) 938-1555 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS FOEIL, M-B, FAROLLAN CAPITAL.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : Defendant Joseph Albanese Incorporated's motion to strike portions of Plaintiff's unverified amended complaint is granted as to the attorney's fees request and denied as to punitive damages and remedial conduct. The complaint adequately alleges that defendant acted in conscious disregard of the safety of bicyclists. Further information can be obtained through discovery. The remedial conduct allegation is potentially relevant for purposes other than to prove negligence. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7770 =302/HEK
